steps.title: Extract given information from diagram and problem
steps.content:
From the diagram: AC = 28 (the hypotenuse, labeled on the figure)
The diagram shows a right angle at B.
From the problem: AB = AC − 11 = 28 − 11 = 17

steps.title: Identify the similar triangles created by the altitude
steps.content:
When altitude BD is drawn perpendicular to hypotenuse AC from the right angle at B, it creates triangle BDC.

This triangle shares angle C with the original triangle ABC and also contains a right angle (at D).

Since both triangles have a right angle and share angle C, triangle ABC ∼ triangle BDC.

steps.title: Apply the proportionality from similar triangles
steps.content:
From the similarity, corresponding sides are proportional:
$$\frac{BC}{AC} = \frac{BD}{AB}$$

Cross-multiplying:
$$BC \cdot AB = AC \cdot BD$$

Dividing both sides by $BD$, then by $AB$:
$$\frac{BC}{BD} = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{28}{17}$$
